records.name: Mynet

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2021 (declining off prior peak — partial coverage):* stock ¥535 (Dec 21) – ¥949 (Jan 14) per Nikkei 10-year history; Jan peak ¥949 was the highest in the 5-year window, declined to ¥535 by year-end. FY12/2021 full-year financials not surfaced in this round — partial. Stock-move catalyst for the Jan→Dec decline: not sourced — catalyst needs_human. Sign summary: shares fell sharply from ¥949 Jan peak to ¥535 Dec low.

records.name: Mynet

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2022 (operating loss year — deliberate investment that didn't pay off):* stock ¥354 (Dec 23) – ¥617 (Jan 4); continued decline. **FY12/2022 actual: operating LOSS ¥399M** — driven by Fantasy Sports investment acceleration + Club DX investment + corporate cost increases per gamebiz. Sign summary: operating profit fell to a ¥399M loss; revenue declined; shares fell to ¥354 low. 業績 ✕ (genuine loss year, investment program did not yet pay off) / 株価 ✕.

records.name: Mynet

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation: *2023 (turn-to-black year — restructuring-margin-recovery pattern):* stock ¥268 (Dec 27) – ¥451 (Sep 4); year-end multi-year low despite operational improvement. **FY12/2023 actual: revenue ¥9.4B (-10.8% YoY), operating profit ¥100M (turned to black vs ¥399M loss prior period), recurring profit ¥80M (vs ¥419M loss prior period), net profit ¥70M (vs ¥1.633B loss prior period)** per gamebiz. Cost discipline + portfolio rationalization. Sign summary: revenue fell -10.8% YoY; operating profit turned to black at ¥100M from a ¥399M loss prior period; net profit turned to black at ¥70M from a ¥1.633B loss prior period; shares fell to ¥268 low. **Split-signal year: revenue ✕ / profit ◎ (turn-to-black recovery)**, parallel to Square Enix 2025 restructuring-margin-recovery pattern. 業績 split / 株価 ✕ (stock didn't recognize the turnaround).

records.rawExplanation: *2024 (continued recovery — OP +154%):* stock ¥208 (Aug 5) – ¥528 (Feb 20); stock hit multi-year low ¥208 in August despite strong fundamentals. **FY12/2024 actual: revenue ¥8.846B (+1.5% YoY), operating profit ¥428M (+154.2%)** — Game segment ¥8.4B (-2.2%) with OP ¥345M (+168.5%), Non-game B2B segment revenue ¥489M with OP ¥82M (new growth driver). Both segments contributed to OP expansion. Sign summary: revenue rose +1.5% YoY; operating profit rose +154.2%; net profit rose strongly; shares fell to ¥208 multi-year low (stock did NOT recognize the +154% OP improvement). **The widening gap between accelerating fundamentals and the depressed stock is the under_priced_de_rated_grower pattern at micro-cap scale** (similar to PLAID 2024, Rakus 2024). 業績 ◎ (rev + profit growing) / 株価 ✕✕ (de-rated grower).

records.name: Mynet

reasons.notes: Convergence-test candidate — human review needed before consolidation.

records.rawExplanation:
*2025 (guidance reduced + stock new low — fragile recovery):* stock ¥174 (Apr 7) – ¥357 (Feb 13); year-low ¥174 was the lowest in the 5-year window. **FY12/2025 revised guidance: revenue ¥7.5B (-15.2% YoY), operating profit ¥375M (-12.4%)** — case timing shifted to next year reduced the top line, but secondary-domain title profitability came in BETTER than originally planned, improving margins. H1 actual: revenue ¥3.939B (-15.8%), OP ¥242M (-21.6%). Sign summary: revenue fell -15.2% YoY (guidance); operating profit fell -12.4% (guidance); H1 net profit forecast lifted from ¥166M to ¥220M on profitability improvements; shares fell to ¥174 multi-year low. 業績 mixed (revenue ✕, profit-quality improving via title-margin focus) / 株価 ✕.

Cautious side: persistent revenue decline 2022-2025 (every year except FY24); business model (acquiring + operating existing game IPs) is naturally a melting-ice-cube without new IP wins; Fantasy Sports + Club DX investment did not produce returns; micro-cap (~¥2.1B) means thin trading and price can move sharply on small news; B2B Non-Game segment is small (~¥500M) but the only growth lane.

Boundary note for Nakamachi: Mynet is a mobile-game OPERATOR / platform business — not a content creator. Acquires existing IPs and extracts value via operations. Leans platform-side of the platform-vs-content boundary; closer to a SaaS-style recurring-revenue business than to a content-cycle gaming company. Worth confirming this is the side of the line you want in the deployment universe.

Sources:
- https://gamebiz.jp/news/400862 (FY12/2024 通期決算 + FY12/2025 guidance + segment detail)
- https://gamebiz.jp/news/418339 (FY12/2025 revised guidance + reasoning)
- https://gamebiz.jp/news/364413 (FY12/2022 OP loss ¥399M + investment driver)
- https://gamebiz.jp/news/405737 (Q1 2025 update — secondary-domain profitability holding)
- https://www.nikkei.com/article/DGXZRST0526951V21C25A2000000/ (FY12/2025 net profit forecast revision upward)
- https://www.buffett-code.com/company/3928/ (company profile + segments)
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/company/history/yprice/?scode=3928 (Nikkei 10-year price history — all per-year highs/lows)
